reasoning: The FedRAMP SI-16 control focuses on memory protection, which can be mapped to Kubernetes through ensuring that pods and nodes have security contexts and configurations that protect against memory-based attacks. This includes enabling security features like memory protection mechanisms (e.g., ASLR, DEP) at the node level, and enforcing policies that ensure these protections are not bypassed at the pod level. Admission controllers can be used to enforce these policies.
title: Memory Protection
